revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 豊田神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=江戸川区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[江戸川区]][[東瑞江]]1-18-1 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[経津主神|経津主命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''豊田神社'''（とよたじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[江戸川区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == 創建年代は不明である。元々は「神明社」といい、下鎌田村の鎮守であった<ref name="史跡散歩">内田定夫 著『江戸川区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド23)』[[学生社]]、1992年、89-90p</ref>。
[[明治時代]]の[[神仏分離]]政策により、[[別当寺]]だった長寿院が廃寺になり、長寿院跡地に移転して「豊田神社」に改称した<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref>。
境内には、「下鎌田の富士塚」と呼ばれる[[富士塚]]がある。[[1916年]]（大正5年）に[[富士講]]「下鎌田割菱八行講」によって築造されたもので、江戸川区の有形文化財に登録されている<ref name="史跡散歩"></ref><ref name="江戸川区">[https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/036.html 下鎌田の富士塚]江戸川区教育委員会事務局教育推進課</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[瑞江駅]]より徒歩10分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *内田定夫 著『江戸川区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド23)』[[学生社]]、1992年
== 関連項目 == * [[富士塚]]

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

== Notes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992.
* {{cite book|和書|title=Edo River Ward History|volume=Volume 3|chapter=Folk Culture and Nature, Section 2: Shrines and Temples, Part 1: Origins of Shrines, No. 15 Toyota Shrine|page=475|chapterurl=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=9|url=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=1|publisher=Edogawa Ward|date=1976-03|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区史}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/shite/035.html|title=Toyota Shrine Forest|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/036.html|title=Shimokamata Fujizuka|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}}}

== Related Reading ==

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]'s [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the [[:en:Fujizuka|Fuji mound]] known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the [[:en:Fujiko (religion)|Fujikō]] group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from [[:en:Mizue Station|Mizue Station]]. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

== Notes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992.
* {{cite book|和書|title=Edo River Ward History|volume=Volume 3|chapter=Folk Culture and Nature, Section 2: Shrines and Temples, Part 1: Origins of Shrines, No. 15 Toyota Shrine|page=475|chapterurl=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=9|url=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=1|publisher=Edogawa Ward|date=1976-03|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区史}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/shite/035.html|title=Toyota Shrine Forest|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/036.html|title=Shimokamata Fujizuka|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}}}

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}'s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fuji mound}} known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|WD=Q11456201|lt=Fujikō}} group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from {{ill|Mizue Station|en|Mizue Station|WD=Q1040898|lt=Mizue Station}}. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

== Notes ==

{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992.
* {{cite book|和書|title=Edo River Ward History|volume=Volume 3|chapter=Folk Culture and Nature, Section 2: Shrines and Temples, Part 1: Origins of Shrines, No. 15 Toyota Shrine|page=475|chapterurl=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=9|url=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=1|publisher=Edogawa Ward|date=1976-03|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区史}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/shite/035.html|title=Toyota Shrine Forest|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}}}
* {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.edogawa.tokyo.jp/e_bunkazai/bunkazai/toroku/036.html|title=Shimokamata Fujizuka|website=Edogawa Ward Cultural Assets and Historic Sites|accessdate=2020-05-31|date=2019-01-31|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}}}

== Related Reading ==
* {{cite book|和書|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki]]|volume=Volume 29, Katsushika District 10|chapter=Shimokamata Village|id={{NDLJP|763979/65}}|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geography Bureau|date=1884-06}}
''' ''Katori Shrine'': "The village guardian, managed by Chōju-in Temple."
''' ''Chōju-in Temple'': "Shingon Sect of Shingi Line, subordinate to Shōfuku-ji in Kamikamata Village."

The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}'s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fuji mound}} known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|WD=Q11456201|lt=Fujikō}} group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from {{ill|Mizue Station|en|Mizue Station|WD=Q1040898|lt=Mizue Station}}. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

'''Toyota Shrine''' (豊田神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Higashi-Mizue, Edogawa City, Tokyo.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}'s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|WD=Q5507555|lt=Fuji mound|1=Fujizuka}} known as "Shimokamata no Fujizuka," which was constructed in 1916 by the {{ill|Fujiko (religion)|en|Fujiko (religion)|WD=Q11456201|lt=Fujikō|1=Fujiko (religion)}} group, "Shimokamata Waribishi Hakkō-kō." The mound is registered as a tangible cultural asset of Edogawa City.<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}.

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Toyota Shrine Forest''' - Designated as a natural monument of Edogawa City on February 8, 1982.{{sfn|江戸川区 豊田神社の社叢}}
* '''Shimokamata Fujizuka''' - Registered as a tangible folk cultural property of Edogawa City on March 15, 1983.{{sfn|江戸川区 下鎌田の富士塚}}

== Access ==
* 7-minute walk from {{ill|Mizue Station|en|Mizue Station|WD=Q1040898|lt=Mizue Station|1=Mizue Station}}. {{OSM経路図|start=35.69304,139.89765|goal=35.68944,139.89882|name=Route Map}}.

* {{cite book|和書|title=Edo River Ward History|volume=Volume 3|chapter=Folk Culture and Nature, Section 2: Shrines and Temples, Part 1: Origins of Shrines, No. 15 Toyota Shrine|page=475|chapterurl=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=9|url=https://adeac.jp/edogawa-lib/viewer/mp100030-100005/edogawa306/?p=1|publisher=Edogawa Ward|date=1976-03|ref={{sfnref|江戸川区史}}}}

The exact date of the shrine's establishment is unknown. Originally named "Shimmeisha," it served as the guardian deity of {{ill|Shimokamata Village|ja|下鎌田村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokamata Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Historical Walk">Uchida Sadao, ''Edo River Ward Historical Walk'' (Tokyo Historical Guide 23), Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 89–90.</ref>{{sfn|江戸川区史}}.

During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}'s {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} policy, the shrine's affiliated {{ill|Bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}, Chōju-in Temple, was abolished, and the shrine was moved to the temple's former grounds and renamed "Toyota Shrine."<ref name="Historical Walk"/> {{sfn|江戸川区史}}.
